name: Kapala Bowl

visual_attributes.morphology: bowl-shaped structure made from a human skull
distinctive_features: mounted with precious metals and jewels
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: approximately the size of a human cranium
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: skull cup with a handle and a lid, often placed on a triangular pedestal

domain_specific_visual.SYMBOLIC_ENGRAVINGS: depictions of deities and tantric symbols

functional_attributes.primary_function: ritual implement for offerings and meditation

domain_specific_functional.TANTRIC_RITUAL_USE: used in higher tantric meditation and libations

relationships.description: Used in rituals and offerings in Tibetan Buddhism
Depicted with deities like Kali, Durga, and Shiva

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the transformation of duality into non-dual awareness

contrastive_constraints: NOT made of ceramic or clay
NOT used for mundane purposes like eating or drinking
